samples.text: Recursion is when a function calls itself. Every recursive function needs a base case to stop the recursion; without understanding the base case you cannot reason about recursion correctly.

a.name: Base Case
a.summary: The terminating condition that stops a recursive function from calling itself.

b.name: Recursion
b.summary: A technique where a function calls itself to solve smaller subproblems.

samples.text: Stacks and queues are both linear collections. A stack is LIFO and a queue is FIFO; they are often taught together as contrasting abstract data types but neither requires the other.
Variables store values. Photosynthesis is a biological process in plants. These topics come from unrelated domains.

a.name: Photosynthesis
a.summary: How plants convert light to chemical energy.

b.name: Variables
b.summary: Named storage locations in a program.

samples.text: Before you can understand gradient descent you must understand what a derivative is, since gradient descent moves along the negative gradient, which is a vector of partial derivatives.

a.name: Derivatives
a.summary: The rate of change of a function with respect to its input.

b.name: Gradient Descent
b.summary: An optimization method that follows the negative gradient.
